close_reason: Same-position contraction fallback added to _eval_spin_scalar. The residual dS gauge violation is a theoretical issue (flat projectors on dS kernel), not a code bug.
description: When projecting kernels extracted from MSS (de Sitter) expressions, spin_project produces terms with free indices (e.g. k^o k^o, g^{oo}) instead of a fully scalar result. Root cause: the projector's internal dummy indices (from theta/omega) collide with coefficient indices after ensure_no_dummy_clash. At Λ=0 (flat limit) this doesn't manifest because Λ-dependent terms vanish. At finite Λ, the collision creates uncontractable free index pairs. Diagnosis: inspecting spin_project(K_EH_dS, :spin2) shows 3 of 7 terms have 2 free Up indices named 'o'. Fix needed: ensure all dummy indices in the projector are unique w.r.t. the full coefficient expression.
notes: The residual g^{oo} issue is handled by _eval_spin_scalar fallback contraction. The REAL blocker for dS crosscheck is that flat Barnes-Rivers projectors give wrong results on dS kernels — the projectors need MSS adaptation (background curvature modifies the eigenvalue spectrum). This is a theoretical extension (dS-adapted spin projectors), not a bug in the existing code.
title: spin_project has index collision on dS kernels — free indices leak into scalar result

close_reason: Fixed by clean-room extract_kernel_direct: bypasses both simplify and to_fourier for bilinear forms
description: The simplify pipeline (canonicalize + collect_terms) merges ΓΓ and ∂Γ terms during term collection. After merging, bilinear terms end up wrapped inside derivative nodes as ∂(h₁×stuff×h₂), which cannot be decomposed into left-h × right-h × coefficient. This is the core reason the Fourier→extract_kernel pipeline gives wrong spin projections. Workaround: extract kernel from RAW (unsimplified) perturbation engine output, or pre-simplify individual factors before forming bilinear products.
title: simplify merges terms into ∂(bilinear) that breaks kernel extraction

Modify src/algebra/ibp.jl to support this. The GHY term is essential for well-posed variational problems with boundaries. Test: verify δ(S_EH + S_GHY)/δg = 0 gives correct Einstein equations. ~200 lines.
title: Implement GHY boundary term extraction

description: After fixing the √g coefficient bug, run the full covariant cross-check pipeline: expand_perturbation → commute_covds → to_fourier → extract_kernel → spin_project. Test on EH term first (κR), verify gauge sectors vanish (spin-1=0, spin-0w=0) at multiple (k²,Λ) points. Then extend to R³ cubic: extract BC parameters from form factor zeros, compare against bc_R3() predictions to ±5%. Files: examples/15_perturbation_spectrum_crosscheck.jl, src/action/kernel_extraction.jl.
notes: Session 32 final: The dS crosscheck is blocked by the need for dS-adapted Barnes-Rivers projectors. Flat Barnes-Rivers applied to dS kernel gives spin-2 = 2.5 - 5Lambda (wrong), while BC predicts 2.5 (constant). The flat crosscheck is COMPLETE and fully validated.
title: Validate dS crosscheck via √g perturbation (Approach 1)

close_reason: Sign convention documented in HANDOFF and tested in test_kernel_extraction.jl (5 parameter combinations)
description: The full kinetic kernel for S = ∫√g(κR + α₁R² + α₂Ric²) on flat background is K = κ·K_EH - 2α₁·K_{R²} - 2α₂·K_{Ric²}. The -2 factor was verified against Buoninfante form factors (2012.11829 Eq.2.13). The perturbation engine pipeline uses: K_EH = extract_kernel_v2(h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab}), K_R² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹R)²), K_Ric² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹Ric)²). The -2 sign is NOT a bug in the extraction; it comes from the relationship between the action and the kinetic operator. Must add tests verifying all 4 combinations (κ only, +R², +Ric², both) against analytic form factors.
title: Document and test -2 sign convention for R²/Ric² kernel contributions

description: Create test/test_ds_spin_projectors.jl. Tests: (1) At Λ=0, dS projectors exactly reproduce flat results for all 4 spins. (2) For EH action (κR), spin-2 coefficient = κ/2 independent of Λ at Λ=0.1,0.5,1.0. (3) Gauge sectors (spin-1, spin-0w) vanish for EH at all Λ. (4) String-match spin-0s coefficient against paper formula. (5) For R² and Ric² terms, match form factors against PSALTer Table values. Ground truth = direct comparison against analytic expressions from arXiv:2406.09500. Do NOT modify test/runtests.jl (will be added after merge).
title: Validate dS spin projectors: string-match against paper ground truth

close_reason: Fixed by clean-room extract_kernel_direct: bypasses both simplify and to_fourier for bilinear forms
description: In a quadratic action ∫dx h₁(x) K h₂(x), the two fields carry opposite momenta (+k and -k). The to_fourier function replaces ALL ∂→k regardless of which field the derivative acts on. This is correct only when each derivative acts on a different field (symmetric: n_L=n_R). For asymmetric terms like h₁(∂²h₂), uniform-k gives k² but the correct result is -k² (the (-ik)² = -k² from the right field momentum). The phase correction is (-1)^{n/2+n_R} where n=n_L+n_R.
title: to_fourier uniform-k gives wrong signs for bilinear forms with asymmetric derivatives

close_reason: Root cause fixed: clean-room extract_kernel_direct with correct two-momentum phase
description: CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS (session 28): The crosscheck failure is NOT in collect_terms/canonicalize. spin_project correctly handles shared dummies and mixed index positions (verified with FP kernel). The real bug: δ²R from the perturbation engine has A-type coefficient 5/4 (h^{ab}h_{ab}k²) instead of expected 1/2, giving spin-2=6.25 instead of 2.5. Likely a convention mismatch between expand_perturbation and the crosscheck formula, or a bug in the second-order inverse metric expansion. See HANDOFF-next-session.md for deep research plan.
notes: Session 30: Root cause fully identified. Two interacting bugs: (1) to_fourier mishandles ∂(product) for quadratic forms — treats as k×product instead of distributing via Leibniz with correct momentum routing; (2) contract_metrics can't contract metrics with TDeriv partners. δ²Ric has 2 unexpanded ∂(h×∂h) terms giving spurious spin-2=2.5 and 18 terms with uncontracted g's giving 1.25. Total 3.75 vs correct 0. Simplest fix: Option D — detect and drop total-divergence terms before Fourier transform. See HANDOFF-next-session.md for full analysis.
title: δ²R perturbation engine gives wrong spin-2 coefficient (misdiagnosed as canonicalizer bug)

close_reason: Closed
description: In examples/15_perturbation_spectrum_crosscheck.jl line 49, the coefficient 1//8 in the (tr h)² term of sqrt_g_correction() should be 1//4. This is derived from δ²(√g·L)/√g₀ = δ²L + (1/2)tr(h)·δL + L₀·[−(1/2)h^{ab}h_{ab} + (1/4)(tr h)²]. The wrong coefficient distorts the kinetic structure, pulling weight from spin-2 into gauge sectors, causing non-zero spin-1 and spin-0w. Fix the coefficient, then validate FP sanity check at Λ→0: Tr(K·P²)≈2.5k², spin-1=0, spin-0w=0.
title: Fix √g coefficient bug in perturbation crosscheck

close_reason: HANDOFF-next-session.md rewritten with correct formulas and pipeline
description: The HANDOFFs from sessions 30-31 use the formula δ²(√gR) = δ²R + (1/2)tr(h)δ¹R. While algebraically correct, this decomposition is WRONG for kernel extraction because the cross term (1/2)tr(h)δ¹R has n_L=0,n_R=2 and its spin projection gives (0, 1.5) not the expected (1.25, -0.5). The CORRECT approach for the EH bilinear form is h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} = h^{ab}(δ¹R_{ab} - (1/2)g_{ab}δ¹R), which gives the FP kernel directly. Verified: spin-2=2.5, spin-0s=-1.0.
title: HANDOFF documents wrong formula for EH bilinear: use h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} not δ²R+cross

Use symbolic_diagonal_metric() from components/symbolic_metric.jl with Symbolics.jl. Register the metric via define_metric!. Return a NamedTuple (metric=SymbolicMetric, free_functions=[A,B], radial_coord=r). Test: verify Christoffel symbols match hand-computation for general A(r),B(r). ~150 lines.
title: Implement spherical symmetry metric ansatz generator

close_reason: define_killing! now auto-registers Killing equation as rewrite rules. check_killing() added. All tests pass.
description: Extend define_killing\!(reg, :ξ; manifold, metric) to automatically register the Killing equation £_ξ g_{ab} = ∇_a ξ_b + ∇_b ξ_a = 0 as a RewriteRule. Currently define_killing\! only stores metadata but doesn't enforce the equation. Use make_rule() with the Killing equation LHS → 0. Also add check_killing(reg, :ξ, expr) that simplifies an expression using Killing rules. Located in src/gr/killing.jl. ~80 lines.
title: Auto-register Killing equation as rewrite rules
